REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, PETITIONER YUYUN HUANG'S PETITION FOR WRIT OF ADMINISTRATIVE MANDAMUS [CCP 1094.5] IS DENIED. THE RENT BOARD DID NOT EXCEED ITS JURISDICTION AND DID NOT ABUSE ITS DISCRETION IN DETERMINING THAT A TENANCY WAS CREATED UNDER THE SAN FRANCISCO RENT ORDINANCE. THE RENT BOARD DID NOT MAKE ANY DETERMINATIONS AS TO THE VALIDITY OF THE LEASE AND THIS ISSUE IS NOT PROPERLY BEFORE THE COURT ON THE WRIT PROCEEDINGS. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: RONALD E. QUIDACHAY, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED (D501)

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandamus [Ccp 1094.5]
Off Calendar Feb-10-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Richard Beckman, Esq (415-240-4087) of Beckman Blair LLP for Petitioner/Moving party YUYUN HUANG.
Wayne Snodgrass, Deputy City Attorney (415-554-4675) for Respondent/opposing party Residential Stabilization and Arbitration Board of the CCSF.
Wendy Hillger, Esq. (415-795-3579) of Ad Astria for Real Party in Interest Hingorani et al.

The Court indicates the ruling and has concurred with the opposing side; the City has set forth the issues clearly. ARGUED. Plaintiff argues jursidictional issues, fraud. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PETITIONER YUYUN HUANG's Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandamus [Ccp 1094.5] is DENIED. The Rent Board did not exceed its jurisdiction and did not abuse its discretion in determining that a tenancy was created under the San Francisco Rent Ordinance. The Rent Board did not make any determinations as to the validity of the lease and this issue is not properly before the Court on the writ proceedings. Prevailing party to submit order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported (D501)
